agent_notes: Quality-over-volume run: Tower Hamlets has many culturally strong venues, but the PAINTS backup rule, universal walk-in rule, and strict Books & Coffee definition reduce the qualifying set below the 30-35 target.
Creative Walks are deliberately sparse: Victoria Park is the only Tower Hamlets park in the PAINTS safety list; Tower Hamlets Cemetery Park and Mudchute were excluded/paused by the v6 brief.
Opening hours and walk-in/group tolerance are cited where official sources state them; remaining operational details are marked UNVERIFIED - host must confirm before publishing.

venues.walk_in_policy: Bookings available; walk-ins for 8-12 not explicitly guaranteed. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good wine-bar table format but group seating must be confirmed.
venues.capacity_notes: Small, atmospheric wine bar; use early evening or Sunday afternoon for lower pressure.
venues.why_fits_paints: A benchmark East London wine bar with proper bottle-list depth and enough lore to sustain a show-and-tell format. It feels grown-up without becoming corporate, and the railway-arch/Hackney Road setting keeps it textured. Press recognition raises confidence, but popularity creates peak-time risk.
venues.anecdote_story: Michael Sager and Charlotte Wilde's bar is widely credited with helping make London fine wine feel casual and accessible rather than hushed and formal.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Fine, low-intervention and classic wines by the glass/bottle with small plates.
venues.safety_notes: Hackney Road/Hoxton route has strong evening footfall; host should shepherd attendees to Hoxton Overground or Bethnal Green routes after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 8 min walk by Google Maps search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Top-list obviousness penalty and limited group walk-in certainty; confirm table availability before matching.

venues.venue_name: Cave Cuvee / Shop Cuvee Bethnal Green
venues.opening_hours: Shop/bar hours vary by source; CAMRA says shop open seven days and bar opens from 14:00; host must confirm exact bar hours.
venues.walk_in_policy: Love Bethnal Green explicitly says they welcome walk-ins; group of 8-12 still UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Basement bar is intimate; suitable only if the host confirms a corner/table.
venues.capacity_notes: Small subterranean natural wine bar under the shop.
venues.why_fits_paints: Natural wine, bottle-shop browsing, and a slightly hidden basement make this a strong conversation venue. The format lets guests pick a bottle label or producer story as a prompt. It is more niche and less overexposed than the headline East London wine bars.
venues.anecdote_story: Top Cuvee expanded east with a Bethnal Green bottle shop and Paris-style underground wine bar after its pandemic-era delivery and natural-wine following grew.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Natural wine bottles, chilled reds, orange wine, pet-nat, small plates and bar snacks.
venues.safety_notes: Bethnal Green Road is busy and well lit; use Bethnal Green Underground/Overground routes, with host-managed exits after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 8 min walk by Google Maps search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Exact current hours are not cleanly published on the main shop page; confirm by phone/Instagram before event listing.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists Mon closed, Tue-Fri 17:00-23:00, Sat 14:00-23:00, Sun 14:00-21:00.
venues.walk_in_policy: Reservations offered; walk-ins/group tolerance UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely workable because it is a winery bar under railway arches, but must confirm seating.
venues.capacity_notes: Urban winery/wine bar with production-story angle; group should avoid peak Friday.
venues.why_fits_paints: A functioning urban winery gives the host a concrete object-story: London wine made in arches rather than imported ritual. It is visually distinctive and less generic than most cocktail bars. The operational hours are clear, though group walk-in needs confirmation.
venues.anecdote_story: Renegade frames itself around making London wine from grapes sourced across Europe, turning an East End arch into an urban winery rather than just a bar.
venues.serve_or_on_view: London-made wines, wine flights, bottles and bar pours.
venues.safety_notes: Bethnal Green station routes are straightforward; Gales Gardens approach should be checked by host for lighting after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 9 min walk by Google Maps search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Confirm whether a spontaneous group of 8-12 can be seated without splitting.

venues.opening_hours: Site/Visit London indicate pub/rooftop operation; rooftop walk-ins for drinks when space allows 12:00-21:30 Mon-Sat.
venues.walk_in_policy: Walk-ins welcome when space allows; group of 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm. Demoted for peak fragility.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Ground-floor pub likely better than rooftop for group conversation.
venues.capacity_notes: Pub, restaurant, rooms and rooftop garden; avoid rooftop-only plan in bad weather.
venues.why_fits_paints: The herb-garden rooftop and Culpeper historical hook give the host more to work with than a standard pub. It is photogenic without being a hotel-bar default. It is demoted because walk-in table certainty for 8-12 is not guaranteed.
venues.anecdote_story: Named for Nicholas Culpeper, the 17th-century herbalist and physician associated with Spitalfields herbal medicine lore.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Pub drinks, wine, seasonal food and rooftop garden setting.
venues.safety_notes: Commercial Street and Aldgate East are busy; Spitalfields night-time crowding is a watchout but transit access is strong.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 7 min walk by Google Maps search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Weather and walk-in competition; hold as Tier B unless PAINTS can reserve space.

venues.opening_hours: UNVERIFIED - host must confirm current opening hours from venue site/Google before publishing.
venues.walk_in_policy: Reservations recommended; walk-in group of 8-12 UNVERIFIED - host must confirm. Demoted.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Better for a hosted pub-table session than a spontaneous large walk-in.
venues.capacity_notes: Historic pub/gastropub; dining demand can make casual drinks fragile.
venues.why_fits_paints: A serious East End pub with food-world validation and a restrained, story-rich room. It fits the character-gastropub branch of Show & Tell Drinks. The operational risk is that its reputation makes unbooked group seating uncertain.
venues.anecdote_story: A Victorian public house revived as a modern gastropub, now known for British cooking as much as pints.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Pub drinks, wine and seasonal British food.
venues.safety_notes: Hackney Road has good footfall and transit; host should route guests to Hoxton or Bethnal Green after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 2 min walk by Google Maps search.
venues.risks_watchouts: May be too food-led/booked-up for the PAINTS walk-in model.

venues.opening_hours: Official pub site lists Mon-Wed 15:00-23:00, Thu-Sat 12:00-23:00, Sun 12:00-22:30.
venues.walk_in_policy: Instagram says tables are left free for walk-ins if unable to book; group of 8-12 UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Potentially suitable at off-peak times; upstairs gallery adds texture.
venues.capacity_notes: Neighbourhood pub with The Approach gallery upstairs.
venues.why_fits_paints: The pub-plus-gallery combination is exactly the sort of low-key cultural overlap PAINTS can use. It is less polished than the wine bars, which may help conversation. It is Tier B because the drinks proposition is not as wine-led as the brief's ideal.
venues.anecdote_story: The Approach Gallery has operated above the tavern for over 25 years and is known for artists who later gained wider recognition.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Pub drinks and contemporary art upstairs when exhibitions are open.
venues.safety_notes: Residential Bethnal Green route; safe for daytime/early evening, but host should avoid late solo dispersal through quiet side streets.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 7 min walk by Google Maps search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Category fit depends on pairing pub discussion with gallery context; confirm gallery opening if using that angle.

venues.opening_hours: Official contact page lists Wed/Thu 12:00-19:00, Fri/Sat 12:00-20:00, Sun 12:00-17:00.
venues.walk_in_policy: Wine shop/record shop format suggests walk-in retail, but sitting group of 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Best as a short tasting/show-and-tell stop, not a full seated event.
venues.capacity_notes: Record and wine shop, limited dwell space.
venues.best_time_slot: Friday 16:00-18:00 or Sunday after Columbia Road Market thins.
venues.why_fits_paints: Records, hi-fi and natural wine give instant show-and-tell objects. Columbia Road adds an unusually local texture outside the usual Bethnal Green bar run. It is Tier B because capacity and seated group stay are uncertain.
venues.anecdote_story: From the Brilliant Corners orbit, Idle Moments blends wine culture with listening-room/record-shop sensibility.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Natural wine shelves, records and hi-fi culture.
venues.safety_notes: Columbia Road is high footfall on Sundays; quieter evenings require host-led route planning to Hoxton or Bethnal Green.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 8 min walk by Google Maps search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Not a guaranteed seated venue; only use if the operator agrees to host group browsing/tasting.

venues.opening_hours: Official site and Visit London list Sunday 08:00-15:00 approximately.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access street market; no ticket. Meeting point walk-in must be confirmed.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Strong if hosted early; too crowded at midday.
venues.capacity_notes: Narrow street market; split into pairs for browsing, regroup at agreed corners.
venues.why_fits_paints: It is sensory, storied and instantly visual, with independent shops framing the flower stalls. The best PAINTS version is not a generic tourist browse but a guided look at plants, shopfronts and East End market culture. It carries an obviousness penalty but still clears the bar.
venues.anecdote_story: The market sits among Victorian shopfronts and has become one of London's best-known Sunday rituals, with plant traders and small independents sharing the street.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Flowers, plants, bulbs, independent shops, bakery/coffee stops and street-market patter.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime high-footfall market; crowding/pickpocket awareness at peak.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 15 min by local bus/cycle or longer walk; within market backup relaxed radius by transit.
venues.risks_watchouts: Severe crowding and weather; avoid 11:00-13:00 and set a precise meeting point.

venues.opening_hours: Official visitor page lists Mon/Tue/Wed/Fri 10:00-20:00, Thu 08:00-18:00, Sat 10:00-18:00, Sun 10:00-17:00.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access covered market; individual traders vary.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good operationally because of covered space and multiple regroup points.
venues.capacity_notes: Covered market with food, fashion, art/vinyl traders and seating pressure at lunch.
venues.why_fits_paints: Covered weather resilience makes this one of the safest Market Days picks in the borough. The market has enough food, craft and vintage variety to support a choose-one-object show-and-tell prompt. It is more obvious/commercial than Columbia Road, so it needs a host with taste.
venues.anecdote_story: Spitalfields' market history connects old East End trade routes with the present-day covered market and surrounding Huguenot/warehouse streets.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Food traders, fashion, art, vinyl, antiques days and covered market architecture.
venues.safety_notes: Very strong footfall and transit; watch lunch crowds and bag security.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 9 min walk by Google Maps search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Can feel too polished if the host does not curate a route through the better stalls.

venues.opening_hours: Official Upmarket page lists Mon-Sat 11:00-18:00, Sun 10:00-18:00.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market; no ticket.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good if the host sets short browsing intervals and regroup points.
venues.capacity_notes: Indoor food hall/market with many traders; seating can be scarce.
venues.why_fits_paints: This works when treated as a maker/food crawl rather than a passive market wander. It has enough visual density and stallholder range for conversation prompts. The crowd management challenge is real but manageable early.
venues.anecdote_story: Part of the Truman Markets ecosystem, Upmarket has long functioned as an entry platform for food traders, vintage sellers and small makers.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Street food, handmade goods, vintage/fashion and emerging traders.
venues.safety_notes: Brick Lane has strong footfall; host should avoid late-night dispersal and weekend crowd pinch-points.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 9 min walk by Google Maps search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Popular tourist corridor and queues; start early and keep the group moving.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists Sat 11:00-18:00 and Sun 10:00-18:00.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market; no ticket.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a short craft/vintage loop; seating not the point.
venues.capacity_notes: Covered/warehouse market, good weather resilience.
venues.why_fits_paints: It is a more manageable Brick Lane option than the full street sprawl, with a maker/vintage bias. The indoor feel helps in rain. It is Tier B because the cultural story is thinner than Columbia Road or Spitalfields.
venues.anecdote_story: The market has operated for nearly two decades as part of the Brick Lane/Truman creative-market cluster.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Fashion, vintage, design, craft and food traders.
venues.safety_notes: High-footfall Brick Lane area; bag awareness and precise regroup points needed.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 3 min walk by Google Maps search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Can blur into generic weekend shopping unless the host preselects stalls.

venues.neighbourhood_station: Bow Road / Bow Church / Mile End
venues.opening_hours: Tower Hamlets lists Tuesday, Thursday, Saturday 08:00-18:00; local sources list active trading closer to Tue/Thu 10:00-15:30 and Sat 09:00-16:00.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access street market.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a local-history format, less strong for purely aesthetic browsing.
venues.capacity_notes: Street market; use as a walking market route with hot food/pastry stop.
venues.why_fits_paints: The value is local texture and East End continuity, not polished curation. It counterbalances the Spitalfields/Brick Lane cluster and gives PAINTS a less obvious Bow pick. Tier B because the market mix is practical as well as creative.
venues.anecdote_story: Tower Hamlets notes Roman Road sits on the oldest known trade route in England, and London Museum links it to East London market history.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Clothing, household goods, jewellery, hot food, bread/pastries and local market life.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime market route is appropriate; use Bow Road/Bow Church/Mile End transit in daylight or early evening.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 15 min walk/transit depending on gate; within market backup relaxed radius.
venues.risks_watchouts: Not all stalls are PAINTS-coded; host must curate the route and avoid slow trading days.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists Sunday food market 10:00-16:00, with page hours also showing Sun 10:00-15:00; Instagram/Facebook mention hot food/bar to 17:00.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access park market.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for wandering and casual food; seating depends on weather/park conditions.
venues.capacity_notes: Pedestrianised Nightwalk inside Victoria Park between Bonner Gate and Gore Gate.
venues.why_fits_paints: The market pairs producer food with the only Tower Hamlets park explicitly allowed by PAINTS safety rules. It is easier to host than Brick Lane because the park gives air and regroup space. The aesthetic is warmer than a standard food market.
venues.anecdote_story: The Nightwalk location lets the host connect present-day traders to Victoria Park's civic 'People's Park' history.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Food traders, produce, coffee, hot food and park-side browsing.
venues.safety_notes: Victoria Park is on the PAINTS Safety-First Shortlist; keep to daytime/market hours and main gates.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 15 min walk/transit depending on starting gate; within market relaxed radius.
venues.risks_watchouts: Weather and inconsistent published closing times; confirm Sunday trader list and hours before event.

venues.venue_name: Victoria Park: Pagoda, Lakes and Burdett-Coutts Fountain Loop
venues.neighbourhood_station: Victoria Park / Mile End or Bethnal Green
venues.opening_hours: Park open daily from 07:00 to dusk per Tower Hamlets/Wikipedia source; host should verify seasonal gate closing time.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access park; no ticket.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Strong in daylight with a clear route and weather backup.
venues.capacity_notes: Suggested stops: Bonner Gate/Dogs of Alcibiades, West Lake/Pagoda, Burdett-Coutts Fountain, Old English Garden.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is the cleanest Tower Hamlets Creative Walk because Victoria Park is explicitly on the PAINTS Safety-First Shortlist. The park has real object-stories rather than vague greenery. It supports a gentle two-hour route with cafe fallback.
venues.anecdote_story: The Chinese Pagoda story is especially strong: an entrance feature from the 1842 Hyde Park Chinese Exhibition was moved to Victoria Park, later lost, then reconstructed during restoration.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Designed landscape, pagoda, lakes, fountain, gardens and civic park history.
venues.safety_notes: Victoria Park is on PAINTS Safety-First Shortlist. Daytime only; use main paths and avoid dusk/quiet edges.
venues.backup_walk_time: Within approx. 5 min of west-side start.
venues.risks_watchouts: Weather and gate times; do not route into Tower Hamlets Cemetery Park or Mudchute as backups.

venues.venue_name: Victoria Park Edge and Hertford Union Canal Short Pairing
venues.opening_hours: Open-access route along Victoria Park edge; daylight only per PAINTS v6 canal restriction.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access park/canal edge; no ticket.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable only as a short, daylight hosted walk.
venues.capacity_notes: Suggested stops: Gore Gate/Nightwalk, canal edge view, park lake/garden stop, market/cafe finish if Sunday.
venues.best_time_slot: Sunday 10:30-12:00 when paired with Victoria Park Market.
venues.why_fits_paints: The v6 brief permits Hertford Union Canal only as a Victoria Park edge pairing, so this is intentionally narrow and daytime-only. It adds industrial-waterway texture without violating the safety list. Tier B because canal-edge operational/safety caveats are heavier than the main park loop.
venues.anecdote_story: The route can contrast the People's Park with the working-waterway edge that links Bow/Hackney Wick histories.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Park edge, canal towpath views, market/street-food finish on Sundays.
venues.safety_notes: Allowed only as Hertford Union Canal Victoria Park edge pairing; daytime only, no extended Limehouse Cut/Lee Navigation routing.
venues.backup_venue: Victoria Park Market or Pavilion Cafe depending on start point/day
venues.backup_walk_time: Within approx. 5 min of park-edge start on Sunday; cafe fallback otherwise UNVERIFIED.
venues.risks_watchouts: Do not extend into unapproved canal stretches; weather backup depends on exact start gate.

venues.opening_hours: Official site current pages show July 2026 programme; host must confirm daily public opening hours/date availability before publishing.
venues.walk_in_policy: Gallery generally open/free for many displays; timed/ticketed exhibitions vary. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm event-date entry.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Strong if exhibitions are not at private-view capacity.
venues.capacity_notes: Major public gallery with multiple spaces; use off-peak weekday or Sunday morning.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is the borough's heavyweight gallery pick and the 2026 Backyard Biennial: East programme gives it a specifically local current hook. It has enough institutional history and exhibition density for a two-hour PAINTS day. Obviousness penalty applies, but the current programme justifies inclusion.
venues.anecdote_story: Whitechapel Gallery has a long history of bringing modern and contemporary art to East London and sits at the edge of the City/Shoreditch/Whitechapel cultural corridor.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Backyard Biennial: East runs 15 July-6 September 2026, plus displays and editions programme.
venues.safety_notes: Aldgate East/Whitechapel High Street have strong transit and footfall; host should manage crossings and post-gallery dispersal.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 10 min walk by Google Maps search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Cecilia Vicuna programme ends 5 July 2026 and Backyard Biennial starts 15 July; early July event dates may fall between headline shows.

venues.opening_hours: Official V&A page lists daily 10:00-17:45, galleries close from 17:00, closed 24-26 December.
venues.walk_in_policy: Free general admission; special exhibitions/events may be ticketed. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm event-date timed availability.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for daytime adult creative prompt if positioned around design/material culture rather than children-only framing.
venues.capacity_notes: Large museum; avoid school-holiday peak family hours.
venues.why_fits_paints: The museum is playful but design-rich, and its 2026 programme includes making/illustration/festival hooks. It suits PAINTS if the host frames objects as memory, play and material culture. Operational reliability is high.
venues.anecdote_story: Young V&A reworked the former Museum of Childhood into a design-led museum for children, young people and families, and won Art Fund Museum of the Year 2024.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Collection galleries, design/play objects, Young V&A Summer Festival 2026 and related displays/events.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime Bethnal Green/Cambridge Heath route; use Bethnal Green station and main roads.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 10 min walk by Google Maps search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Family crowds can make adult stranger conversation awkward; choose quieter weekday mornings.

venues.opening_hours: Visit page says during exhibitions Wednesday-Sunday 12:00-18:00, free entry.
venues.walk_in_policy: Free walk-in gallery during exhibitions; group of 8-12 UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for quiet contemporary-art conversation if exhibition scale suits.
venues.capacity_notes: Small gallery; avoid private views and opening weekends.
venues.why_fits_paints: Chisenhale is one of the strongest non-obvious contemporary-art venues in the borough. Its commission model creates enough story around process and production. July 2026 exhibition timing supports a current pick.
venues.anecdote_story: Chisenhale's reputation rests on commissioning artists at important points in their careers, making the gallery itself a story about artistic risk.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Jasper Marsalis, Still Life, listed 10 July-6 September 2026.
venues.safety_notes: Use daytime/early evening; pair with Victoria Park/Bow routes and avoid isolated canal extensions.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 10 min walk by Google Maps search.
venues.risks_watchouts: A 4-9 July event would be before the next listed exhibition opens; host must confirm install/open dates.

venues.opening_hours: Art Fund lists Tuesday-Sunday 10:00-16:00; New Exhibitions lists Wed-Sun 10:00-16:00. Host must confirm exact current schedule.
venues.walk_in_policy: Free public gallery; group of 8-12 UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Strong for a quieter Bow-based gallery day with cafe pairing.
venues.capacity_notes: Free gallery in a former nineteenth-century convent, with Nunnery Cafe nearby.
venues.why_fits_paints: It gives Tower Hamlets geographic balance beyond the usual Spitalfields/Bethnal Green corridor. The former convent setting and Bow Arts ecosystem provide a clear story. The current 2026 community/Bow Open exhibition makes it locally grounded.
venues.anecdote_story: Bow Arts describes the Nunnery as a not-for-profit contemporary art space in a former nineteenth-century convent.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Bow Open x Mile End Community Project presents 'to belong', listed 5 June-30 August 2026.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime Bow Road/Bow Church route; avoid late dispersal through quiet backstreets.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 10 min walk by Google Maps search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Opening-days discrepancy across sources; host should confirm directly with Bow Arts.

venues.opening_hours: Official site and New Exhibitions list Wednesday-Sunday 11:00-18:00.
venues.walk_in_policy: Free gallery; group of 8-12 UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a contained gallery day; rooms are intimate, so stagger viewing.
venues.capacity_notes: Eighteenth-century domestic buildings plus contemporary gallery spaces.
venues.why_fits_paints: Raven Row has the rare mix of domestic-scale architecture and serious contemporary programming. It is quieter than Whitechapel but still culturally substantial. The current exhibition window is ideal for a July run.
venues.anecdote_story: The gallery occupies restored eighteenth-century houses on Artillery Lane, formerly known as Raven Row.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Nomenclature for the Time Being, listed 21 May-6 September 2026.
venues.safety_notes: Spitalfields/Liverpool Street has strong footfall; host should manage narrow streets and crossings.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 10 min walk by Google Maps search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Small rooms mean group movement must be gentle and quiet.

venues.opening_hours: Official calendar lists bookable Silent Day Visit / Silent Night Visit / tours by date; admission not guaranteed without booking.
venues.walk_in_policy: Official contact page says remaining tickets may be sold on the door but admission is not guaranteed; reservation-dependent, so Tier B.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Atmospheric but fragile: silence rules and timed entry make it a special-format PAINTS event only.
venues.capacity_notes: Historic house museum; conversation happens before/after, not during silent visit.
venues.best_time_slot: Friday daytime visit if availability exists.
venues.why_fits_paints: As an object-filled 'still-life drama', this is almost too perfect aesthetically. But it conflicts with the PAINTS walk-in/conversation model because visits are ticketed, silent and capacity-controlled. Use only as a hosted special, not a default match event.
venues.anecdote_story: Dennis Severs created an immersive Huguenot-weaver house world where rooms feel recently vacated, with smell, sound and candlelight carrying the story.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Historic interiors, domestic craft, textiles, furniture and staged material culture.
venues.safety_notes: Spitalfields is well trafficked; use Liverpool Street/Shoreditch High Street routes after evening visits.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 3 min walk by Google Maps search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Reservation/ticketing and silence rules mean PAINTS must pre-book or avoid.

venues.neighbourhood_station: Shoreditch High Street / Bethnal Green Road
venues.opening_hours: Official site lists Shoreditch branch Tuesday-Sunday 11:00-18:30; events may run later.
venues.walk_in_policy: Bookshop/cafe appears open to walk-ins during shop hours; group of 8-12 and 2-hour stay UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Strong cultural fit; confirm table/space if using cafe seating for two hours.
venues.capacity_notes: Queer intersectional bookshop, cafe, bar and community hub with events.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is the borough's best Books & Coffee fit because the literary identity and cafe/bar use are central, not decorative. The shelves give every attendee an immediate prompt. Community-hub warmth suits stranger matching better than a pristine design bookshop.
venues.anecdote_story: The Common Press describes itself as London's queer intersectional bookshop cafe/bar and community hub, with curated books and regular events.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Books across queer, feminist, anti-colonial and community themes; coffee/cafe/bar offer and events.
venues.safety_notes: Bethnal Green Road/Shoreditch High Street route has high footfall; host should manage evening event exits.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 7 min walk by Google Maps search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Events/private hire may affect seating; confirm before scheduling.

venues.opening_hours: Spitalfields Crypt Trust lists Mon-Sat 10:00-18:00, Sun 11:00-17:00.
venues.walk_in_policy: Cafe/bookshop format implies walk-ins; group seating for 8-12 and 2-hour stay UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good at quiet times; smaller than The Common Press.
venues.capacity_notes: Community coffee shop/bookshop selling vintage/second-hand books/items for Spitalfields Crypt Trust.
venues.why_fits_paints: It clears the strict Books & Coffee rule directly: coffee shop plus substantive second-hand books. The social-enterprise story gives the host a human frame without feeling worthy-for-worthy's-sake. It is quieter and more local than Brick Lane browsing.
venues.anecdote_story: Paper & Cup is a Spitalfields Crypt Trust social enterprise supporting people affected by addiction and homelessness.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Coffee, cakes/light cafe offer, second-hand books and vintage items.
venues.safety_notes: Calvert Avenue/Shoreditch route is active by day; use Shoreditch High Street/Hoxton routes after late events.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 7 min walk by Google Maps search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Capacity and current book stock depth should be checked in person before publishing.

rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Explicitly listed as Removed in PAINTS v6 safety list; cannot be sourced or used as backup even though it is locally distinctive.
PAINTS v6 lists Mudchute Park & Farm as PAUSED: daytime family only, so not suitable for the Creative Walks longlist.

rejected_candidates.venue_name: Limehouse Cut / extended Wapping-Limehouse riverside walk
rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: PAINTS v6 explicitly disallows Limehouse Cut and extended Wapping/Limehouse riverside as standalone creative walk routes.
Excellent independent bookshop, but no verified on-site cafe or literal next-door coffee partner under the strict PAINTS Books & Coffee rule.
Strong bookshop and design story, but no verified on-site cafe/coffee partner literally next door; better as backup cultural stop than qualifying Books & Coffee venue.
High-quality independent cocktail bar, but the v6 Show & Tell Drinks rule requires an independent wine bar, natural-wine pub, or character gastro pub; Coupette's fit is cocktail-led.
Characterful Bethnal Green bar with strong poitin/whiskey/music identity, but not a wine bar or character gastro pub under the category's no-exceptions rule.
Useful and locally important street market, but not sufficiently curated/maker/craft-focused for PAINTS compared with Columbia Road, Victoria Park, Spitalfields and Brick Lane options.
